Infinispan.title: Infinispan
Infinispan.description: Defines the configuration for Infinispan, for the cache manager configuration, for the default cache, and for named caches.

Threads.description: The threading subsystem, used to declare manageable thread pools and resources.

group-name.description: Specifies the name of the thread group.

thread-name-pattern.description: The template used to create names for threads. Patterns: %% (percent sign), %t (per-factory thread sequence number), %g (global thread sequence number), %f (factory sequence number), %i (thread ID), %G (thread group name).

ThreadPoolDefinition.description: A thread pool definition. Exactly one pool type should be specified.

BoundedQueueThreadPool.description: A thread pool executor with a bounded queue.

thread-factory.description: Specifies the bean name of a specific thread factory to use.

core-threads.description: Number of threads to keep in the pool.

keepalive-time.description: Amount of time that pool threads should be kept running when idle.

SimpleThreadPool.description: A simple thread pool (scheduled or cached).

thread-factory.description: Specifies the bean name of a specific thread factory to use.

default-cache.description: Indicates the default cache for this cache container.

statistics.description: Determines whether the cache container should collect statistics.

zero-capacity-node.description: If true then no data is stored in this node.

shutdown-hook.description: Behavior of the JVM shutdown hook registered by the cache.

listener-executor.description: Defines the executor used for asynchronous cache listener notifications.

expiration-executor.description: Defines the scheduled executor used for expirations.

non-blocking-executor.description: The name of the executor used for non-blocking operations.

blocking-executor.description: The name of the executor used for blocking operations.

Transport.description: Overrides the transport characteristics for this cache container.

stack.description: Defines the JGroups stack used by the transport.

cluster.description: Defines the name for the underlying group communication cluster.

lock-timeout.description: Timeout to wait to acquire a distributed lock during state transfer or rehashing.

node-name.description: Specifies the name of the current node.

machine.description: Specifies the ID of the machine where the node runs.

rack.description: Specifies the ID of the rack where the node runs.

site.description: The ID of the site where the node runs.

initial-cluster-size.description: The minimum number of nodes that must join the cluster for the cache manager to start.

initial-cluster-timeout.description: The amount of time in milliseconds to wait for a cluster with sufficient nodes to form.

raft-members.description: The list of raft members separated by space.

GlobalSecurity.description: Configures security for this cache container.

cache-timeout.description: The ACL cache timeout in milliseconds.

GlobalAuthorization.description: Configures the global authorization role to permission mapping.

group-only-mapping.description: Specifies whether principal-to-role mapping applies only to group principals.

identity-role-mapper.description: Uses the identity role mapper where principal names are converted as-is into role names.

common-name-role-mapper.description: Uses the common name role mapper which extracts the Common Name from DN format.

cluster-role-mapper.description: Stores the principal to role mappings in the cluster registry.

class.description: Specifies the class name of a custom principal to role mapper implementation.

cluster-permission-mapper.description: Stores role to permission mappings in the cluster registry.

custom-permission-mapper.description: Configures a custom permission mapper implementation.

class.description: Specifies the class name of a custom implementation that maps roles to permissions.

roles.description: Defines authorization roles and their permissions.

permissions.description: Defines the list of permissions for the role.

Serialization.description: Specifies how data serialization will be performed by the cache container.

marshaller.description: Fully qualified name of the marshaller to use.

version.description: Largest allowable version to use when marshalling internal state.

schema-compatibility.description: The kind of compatibility validation that is performed when updating schemas.

AllowList.description: Enables individual classes or regular expressions to be added to the allow list.

class.description: FQN of classes to be added to the allow list.

regex.description: Regex patterns used to determine allow list membership.

prefix.description: Deprecated. Specifies a global name prefix for metrics.

names-as-tags.description: Deprecated. Put the cache manager and cache name in tags.

accurate-size.description: Enables accurate size computation for numberOfEntries statistics.

jvm.description: Whether JVM metrics should be reported.

legacy.description: Whether legacy metrics should be reported.

JMX.description: Enables and configures JMX monitoring and management.

domain.description: JMX domain for published JMX objects.

mbean-server-lookup.description: Class that attempts to locate a JMX MBean server to bind to.

properties.description: JMX properties passed to the MBean Server lookup instance.

GlobalState.description: Defines the global state persistence configuration.

unclean-shutdown-action.description: Defines the action taken when a dangling lock file is found in the persistent global state.

overlay-configuration-storage.description: A persistent configuration storage which saves runtime configurations to the persistent-location.

managed-configuration-storage.description: A persistent configuration storage for managed environments.

custom-configuration-storage.description: Uses a custom configuration storage implementation.

class.description: Class of the custom configuration storage implementation.

GlobalStatePath.description: Defines a filesystem path for global state.

path.description: Defines the path where global state will be stored.

relative-to.description: A property name whose value will be used as the root path.

EvictionContainers.description: Configures container memory for caches that share memory spaces.

EvictionContainerDefinition.description: An eviction container, either count-based or size-based.

name.description: Specifies the name of the memory container.

count.description: Sets the maximum number of entries for the memory container.

size.description: Sets the maximum size of the memory container (e.g. '23MB').

CacheDefinition.description: A named cache definition. Exactly one cache type should be specified.

CommonCacheAttributes.description: Common attributes shared by all cache types.

configuration.description: The name of the cache configuration which this configuration inherits from.

aliases.description: Zero or more cache alias names.

statistics.description: Determines whether the cache should collect statistics.

unreliable-return-values.description: Specifies whether Infinispan is allowed to disregard the Map contract for return values.

simple-cache.description: Use optimized (faster) implementation that does not support transactions, persistence, custom interceptors, indexing, or transcoding.

segments.description: Sets the number of hash space segments per cluster.

key-partitioner.description: The name of the key partitioner class.

owners.description: Number of cluster-wide replicas for each cache entry.

segments.description: Sets the number of hash space segments per cluster.

capacity-factor.description: Controls the proportion of entries that reside on the local node.

l1-lifespan.description: Maximum lifespan of an entry placed in the L1 cache.

l1-cleanup-interval.description: Controls how often a cleanup task to prune L1 tracking data is run.

key-partitioner.description: The name of the key partitioner class.

consistent-hash-factory.description: The factory to use for generating the consistent hash.

ClusteredCacheAttributes.description: Attributes common to clustered cache types.

remote-timeout.description: In SYNC mode, the timeout used to wait for an acknowledgment when making a remote call.

Encoding.description: Defines content type and encoding for keys and values of the cache.

media-type.description: The media type for both keys and values.

Locking.description: The locking configuration of the cache.

isolation.description: Sets the cache locking isolation level.

striping.description: If true, a pool of shared locks is maintained for all entries.

acquire-timeout.description: Maximum time to attempt a particular lock acquisition.

stop-timeout.description: The amount of time to wait for ongoing transactions to finish when a cache is stopped.

locking.description: The locking mode for this cache.

transaction-manager-lookup.description: Fully qualified class name of the TransactionManagerLookup implementation.

complete-timeout.description: The duration in which to keep information about the completion of a transaction.

reaper-interval.description: The time interval at which the thread that cleans up transaction completion information kicks in.

auto-commit.description: If enabled, single operation transactions do not need to be manually started.

recovery-cache.description: Sets the name of the cache where recovery related information is held.

notifications.description: Enables or disables triggering transactional notifications on cache listeners.

max-idle.description: Specifies the maximum amount of time that cache entries can remain idle.

lifespan.description: Specifies the maximum amount of time that cache entries can exist.

interval.description: Specifies the interval between expiration runs.

touch.description: Controls how timestamps get updated for entries in clustered caches.

Memory.description: Controls how the entries are stored in memory.

max-size.description: Defines the size of the data container in bytes (e.g. '1.5GB', '10000000').

max-count.description: Defines the size of the data container by number of entries.

when-full.description: Specifies a strategy for evicting cache entries.

storage.description: Defines the type of memory that the data container uses as storage.

eviction-container.description: Defines the eviction container this cache should use for storage.

Persistence.description: Configures the persistence layer for caches.

passivation.description: Enables passivation so that data is written to cache stores only if it is evicted from memory.

connection-attempts.description: Sets the maximum number of attempts to start each configured CacheWriter or CacheLoader.

availability-interval.description: Specifies the time between availability checks.

AbstractStore.description: Abstract base type for cache stores.

shared.description: Determines if a cache store is shared between cache instances.

preload.description: Pre-loads data into memory from the cache store when the cache starts.

purge.description: Empties the specified cache store at startup.

read-only.description: Prevents data from being persisted to cache stores.

write-only.description: Prevents data from being loaded from cache stores.

transactional.description: Whether the underlying store supports transactions.

max-batch-size.description: Sets the maximum size of a batch to insert or delete.

segmented.description: Configures cache stores to store data in hash space segments.

path.description: Specifies a filesystem directory for data.

open-files-limit.description: Max number of data and index files opened for reading.

compaction-threshold.description: Threshold above which unused space triggers compaction.

FileStoreData.description: Configure where and how entries should be stored.

path.description: A location on disk where the store writes entries.

max-file-size.description: Max size of single file with entries, in bytes.

sync-writes.description: If true, the write is confirmed only after the entry is fsynced on disk.

FileStoreIndex.description: Configure where and how the index should be stored.

path.description: A location where the store keeps index.

max-queue-length.description: Max number of entry writes waiting to be written to the index.

max-node-size.description: Max size of node in bytes.

min-node-size.description: Min size of node in bytes.

WriteBehind.description: Configures cache stores as write-behind instead of write-through.

modification-queue-size.description: Specifies the maximum number of entries in the asynchronous modification queue.

fail-silently.description: Controls how asynchronous write operations take place when cache stores become unavailable.

class.description: Defines the class name of a cache store that implements CacheLoader, CacheWriter, or both.

Indexing.description: Defines indexing options for the cache.

startup-mode.description: Triggers an indexing process when the cache starts.

path.description: Specifies a filesystem path for the index.

indexing-mode.description: Affects how cache operations will be propagated to the indexes.

use-java-embedded-entities.description: Whether indexes will be defined on the Java indexed entities accessible from the class path.

indexed-entities.description: Defines the set of indexed type names.

refresh-interval.description: Interval to reopen the index reader.

commit-interval.description: Amount of time that index changes buffered in memory are flushed to index storage.

ram-buffer-size.description: Maximum amount of memory (MB) for buffering added entries and deletions.

max-buffered-entries.description: Maximum number of entries that can be buffered in-memory.

thread-pool-size.description: Number of threads that execute write operations to the index.

queue-count.description: Number of internal queues to use for each indexed type.

queue-size.description: Maximum number of elements each queue can hold.

low-level-trace.description: Enables low-level trace information for indexing operations.

IndexMerge.description: Defines properties to control the merge of index segments.

max-entries.description: Maximum number of entries that an index segment can have before merging.

factor.description: Number of segments that are merged at once.

min-size.description: Minimum target size of segments, in MB, for background merges.

max-size.description: Maximum size of segments, in MB, for background merges.

max-forced-size.description: Maximum size of segments, in MB, for forced merges.

calibrate-by-deletes.description: Whether the number of deleted entries should be taken into account.

IndexSharding.description: Splitting index data into multiple shards to improve performance.

shards.description: Defines the number of shards. Set to a value strictly greater than 1 to enable sharding.

Query.description: Defines query options for the cache.

default-max-results.description: Limits the number of results returned by a query.

hit-count-accuracy.description: Limit the required accuracy of the hit count for indexed queries.

Backups.description: Defines backup locations for cache data and modifies state transfer properties.

merge-policy.description: Specifies the fully qualified name of a class that implements XSiteEntryMergePolicy or an alias (DEFAULT, PREFER_NON_NULL, PREFER_NULL, ALWAYS_REMOVE).

max-cleanup-delay.description: Specifies the maximum delay between which tombstone cleanup tasks run.

tombstone-map-size.description: Specifies the target number of tombstones to store.

BackupSite.description: Configuration for a specific backup site.

Backup.description: Configures a remote site as a backup location for cache data.

strategy.description: Sets the strategy for backing up to a remote site.

failure-policy.description: Controls how local writes to caches are handled if synchronous backup operations fail.

timeout.description: Specifies timeout for synchronous and asynchronous backup operations.

two-phase-commit.description: Enables two-phase commits for optimistic transactional caches.

failure-policy-class.description: Specifies the class that implements CustomFailurePolicy.

TakeOffline.description: Specifies the number of failures before backup locations go offline.

after-failures.description: Sets the number of consecutive failures before sites go offline.

min-wait.description: Sets the minimum time to wait before sites go offline.

chunk-size.description: Specifies how many cache entries are batched in each transfer request.

timeout.description: The time to wait for the backup site to acknowledge the state chunk.

max-retries.description: Sets the maximum number of retry attempts for push state failures.

wait-time.description: Sets the amount of time to wait between retry attempts.

mode.description: Controls whether cross-site state transfer happens manually or automatically.

BackupFor.description: Defines the local cache as a backup for a remote cache with a different name.

remote-cache.description: Specifies the name of the remote cache that uses the local cache as a backup.

remote-site.description: Specifies the name of the remote site that backs up data to the local cache.

StateTransfer.description: The state transfer configuration for distribution and replicated caches.

enabled.description: If enabled, the cache asks neighboring caches for state when it starts up.

timeout.description: The maximum amount of time to wait for state from neighboring caches.

chunk-size.description: The number of cache entries to batch in each transfer.

await-initial-transfer.description: If enabled, the cache waits for initial state transfer to complete before responding to requests.

PartitionHandling.description: Configures the way this cache reacts to node crashes and split brains.

when-split.description: The type of actions possible when a split brain scenario is encountered.

merge-policy.description: The entry merge policy applied on partition merges. Use built-in values (NONE, PREFERRED_ALWAYS, PREFERRED_NON_NULL, REMOVE_ALL) or a fully qualified class name.

enabled.description: Enables authorization checks for this cache.

roles.description: Sets the valid roles required to access this cache.

enabled.description: Enable or disable tracing on the given cache.

categories.description: Enable or disable tracing for specific categories.
